summary: S007 did not require production code changes; source inspection showed the claim ordering, updateMany race guard, runner-specific lock owner, and queue coordination readiness semantics already existed.

The new tests close the proof gap by covering priority/queuedAt fairness, lost-race no-claim behavior, runner-id lock separation, server-id fallback, and supervisor ready/degraded multi-owner signals.
No default-off, token, schema, endpoint, scheduler, provider, daemon, or web UI behavior changed.
review_gate: Multi-instance safety review: claim fairness, lock ownership, stale owner recovery, and backward-compatible default-off behavior.
